user_prompt: `void CloudUpload::taskMain()` needs to be have the internals of the `for (PendingFile* item = head; item; item = item->next)` loop be broken out in a way that allows for easy calling/testing for just one file
so whatever function you've broken that out into needs another layer of caller that specifies a file, builds a PendingFile structure, and frees the structure when done
also `WiFiClientSecure` needs to be actually secure as much as possible, the destination needs to be authentic
`ClockAgent` must make sure `WiFiClientSecure` works as intended
agent_reply: - Added `CloudUpload::uploadSingleFile(destination, path, timeout_ms)` for easy one-file testing.
- Broke the per-file upload work out into `uploadPendingFile(...)`, shared by both the normal scan loop and the single-file wrapper.
- The single-file path builds a `PendingFile`, calls the shared uploader, then frees it.
- HTTPS is now required for cloud uploads; `http://` is rejected.
- `WiFiClientSecure` now uses the ESP x509 cert bundle via `setCACertBundle(...)`, with hostname verification handled by the secure client connect path.
- `ClockAgent` now pushes its RTC/managed time into system time and exposes `ensureSystemTimeForTls()`, which CloudUpload calls before TLS handshakes.
I did not run PlatformIO, since you said it was busy. Static checks only.
